steps.label: cause
steps.text: Cover crops grow between vineyard rows instead of leaving the inter-row soil bare.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Their roots create channels and add organic matter, while surface vegetation cushions rainfall and some traffic pressure.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Soil aggregation and porosity can improve, reducing compaction when the cover crop and vineyard traffic are managed appropriately.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Managed cover crops can reduce compaction by improving soil structure, organic matter, and pore space.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
